title: Aurum AI attached to live XAUUSD
author: morriase
selftext:
Took a long on XAUUSD yesterday and it’s still open today.

This is from [a deep-learning based system](https://www.auronautomations.app/product/aurum-ai) I’ve been testing that scores both direction *and* expected profitability before entering, then manages exits dynamically instead of fixed TPs.

Do you guys prefer hard targets or adaptive exits on XAU?

Chart for context. Not advice

[Aurum AI attached to live XAUUSD](https://preview.redd.it/zb2vagk6zthg1.png?width=1760&format=png&auto=webp&s=b4f12645f65c9912fce83a93c584ab6cc61b1f41)

comments.author: stereotomyalan
comments.body:
Well, I'd still use TP and SL but for a black swan event. Something like 5 x ATR.

Also I found that it is best to enter a trade on candle close, but when it comes to exits you shouldn't wait for the candle close.

replies.author: morriase
replies.body: Good approach, but isn't 5 × ATR a little too much?

replies.author: stereotomyalan
replies.body:
well, yeah, a bit.   
I use the MT5 optimizer, ATR x 2.5-4.5 seems to be a sweet spot depending on the strategy & asset.

replies.author: morriase
replies.body: Interesting, let me give that a try. I found 3 × ATR to be my sweet spot for this particular system.

replies.author: stereotomyalan
replies.body:
Do you mind me asking your platform? I use the MT5 strategy tester.

I test separately for each year from 2021 to 2026 and then use a GPT (usually gemini) to find the most robust optimizer results.

replies.author: morriase
replies.body: I use the MT5 strategy tester too, mostly I use the optimizer to get the most robust settings for any EA, then I forward test the suitable settings without the optimizer to see if there's any curve fitting. But I prefer to forward test on a live demo to get the best representation of a strategy. Do you use trading bots often?

replies.author: stereotomyalan
replies.body:
yeah, I have one bot working quite well in gold.  
The same strategy works even better in indices, but even their the smallest lot size (0.01) is crazy big. you need \~$10.000 on your account to safely trade indices CFDs.

That's why it's important to use a realistic account size in the strategy tester...

replies.author: morriase
replies.body: Cool. What strategy does it employ? Did you write it yourself?
